content.full_text:
November 2, 1990

California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca 95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

Following attendance at a recent filing officers workshop and receiving the information on the Public Officials Who Manage Public Investments I still have some questions about some of our positions and their involvement in investments.

Our Parks and Recreation Director as well as the Parks and Recreation Commission management, expend and recommend purchase of property for Parks, and construction of park lands.

The Economic Development Director, deals with developers on a large scale, working out financing assistance for developments, whether it is in granting of funds for public improvements or writing down the cost of land owned by the Redevelopment Agency.

The Housing Specialist approves applications for loans and grants of public funds for low to moderate income families for some specific programs related to sewer construction and hook up. She conducts interviews and prepares loan agreements as outlined in the specific program included recording liens on property.

The Community Services Commission is currently working on the construction and expenditure of funds for a regional wastewater treatment plant, they would be making the final recommendation to the City Council.

The Economic Development Advisory Commission works with developers to locate in our community and makes business deals regarding assistance in public improvements and write down on cost of land owned by the Redevelopment Agency. This is advisory, however at what point in time is their recommendation being approved on a regular basis are they considered responsible for the action.

I would appreciate receiving written opinions on these positions in light of the new regulations.

Sincerely Yours,

Dianna M. Higdon, CMC
City Clerk
City of Norco
